summary: High‑energy set with monster Lazy Lightning>Supplication and soaring jams
blurb: Features a blistering Lazy Lightning → Supplication pairing and an explosive Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence. Jerry’s tone is on fire, Keith delivers a groovy The Other One solo, and the dual drummers drive the band throughout. The second set builds into a beautiful Wharf Rat before closing with The Promised Land.
review: July 5, 1978 at the Omaha Civic Auditorium delivered one of those rare summer nights where everything clicked. The first set kicked off with a crisp Sugaree and quickly escalated into a monster Lazy Lightning → Supplication combo that had the crowd buzzing. Jerry’s guitar was drenched in flanger and his tone on both "The Other One" and "Eyes of the World" was described as “killer” and “the sickest ever heard,” while Keith’s surly solo on The Other One added a glossy, late‑Keith sparkle. Phil laid down a thick, solid bass foundation, even stepping out for an extended drums/space segment that let Mickey and Bill rock like they were at Red Rocks. The second set surged through Deal, Samson & Delilah, Ship of Fools into a smoking Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence, then rolled into a post‑drums jam before soaring into a beautifully rendered Wharf Rat. The night closed with an upbeat Promised Land encore, leaving the audience buzzing long after the final chord.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the high‑energy performance, monster Lazy Lightning>Supplication, soaring The Other One, and a smoking Estimated Prophet→Eyes of the World sequence. No consistent negative comments about playing were found; only one minor critique of Iko Iko. This strong consensus yields a large positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear audience tape with good balance and sharp capture of the high‑energy performance, especially the Lazy Lightning → Supplication pairing.

key_highlights: Monster Lazy Lightning → Supplication pair that launches the set.
Soaring The Other One featuring Keith’s groovy solo and Jerry’s flanged tone.
Smoking Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence with an extended post‑drums jam.
Beautifully rendered Wharf Rat capping a high‑energy second set.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is on fire all night, with flanger‑rich licks on Lazy Lightning and a killer solo on Eyes of the World; reviewers call his playing on The Other One "the sickest ever heard."
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides thick, solid bass that anchors the jams; he steps out during the extended drums/space segment but otherwise stays locked in.
band_performance.Keith: Keith shines with a groovy, surly solo on The Other One and delivers clean, glossy playing throughout, typical of his late‑era style.
band_performance.Mickey: Both drummers rock like they’re at Red Rocks; the dual‑drum energy drives the set and the post‑drums jam is especially praised.
band_performance.Billy: Keeps the high‑energy pulse steady, syncing tightly with Mickey and contributing to the powerful rhythmic foundation.
